message:
feat(mux): constraint-aware split allocation with axis constraints system

Replace the simple min-size-only split allocation in the mux tab layout
engine with a full axis-constraints system that honors min, max, and
preferred sizes from PaneConstraints. This enables panes with fixed or
bounded dimensions to participate correctly in split operations and
resize adjustments without being stretched or shrunk beyond their
declared limits.

New types (+163 lines):
  - Axis enum: Width | Height for axis-generic operations
  - AxisConstraints { min, max, preferred }: per-axis size constraint
    with normalized() ensuring min >= 1, max >= min, preferred clamped
  - axis_constraints_from_pane_constraints(): convert PaneConstraints
    into AxisConstraints for a given axis, honoring the fixed flag
    by locking min/max/preferred to the current dimension
  - pane_axis_constraints(): shorthand that reads a pane's constraints
    and current dimensions
  - shared_axis_constraints(): combine two constraints for the axis
    shared between split halves (perpendicular to split direction);
    uses max-of-mins, min-of-maxes, max-of-preferreds
  - additive_axis_constraints(): combine two constraints for the axis
    along the split direction (additive with +1 for separator);
    sums mins, sums maxes, sums preferreds

Refactored functions:
  - compute_axis_constraints(tree, axis): recursive tree walk computing
    AxisConstraints for any subtree, replacing compute_min_size() for
    all callers that need constraint-aware layout
  - split_allocation(): now takes AxisConstraints for first and second
    children instead of bare min values, respecting max constraints
    when computing the feasible allocation range and using preferred
    sizes as the default split point when no explicit preference
  - split_dimension_for_request(): passes AxisConstraints through to
    split_allocation()
  - pane_size_satisfies_constraints(): takes AxisConstraints instead
    of raw PaneConstraints for consistency
  - adjust_x_size() / adjust_y_size(): now query per-subtree
    AxisConstraints to respect max bounds during incremental resize,
    short-circuiting growth when a subtree's max is reached instead
    of unconditionally expanding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
